documentation.content:
The `gridpulse` Cloud Run **service** (the public web tier + JSON API) is returning sustained 5xx responses.

Fires on `run.googleapis.com/request_count{response_code_class="5xx"}` for `service_name="gridpulse"` summed > 25 over 5 minutes. All of the project's other alerting protects the JOB tier; this is the request-path equivalent (#253).

**Runbook:**
1. `curl -s https://gridpulse.kristenmartino.ai/health` — a public liveness `{"status": ...}`; `degraded` points at Redis or stale scoring.
2. From an allowlisted IP, `curl -s '.../health?deep=1'` for the detailed body (Redis / last_scored / forecast_sample).
3. Tail the service logs: `gcloud run services logs read gridpulse --region=us-east1 --limit=200`.
4. Redis cold/flushed → the UI renders the warming state (not a 5xx); a spike of 5xx usually means an unhandled exception or an OOM (check `MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH` / memory). Roll back the revision if a bad deploy: `gcloud run services update-traffic gridpulse --to-revisions=<prev>=100`.

conditionThreshold.filter: resource.type = "cloud_run_revision" AND resource.labels.service_name = "gridpulse" AND metric.type = "run.googleapis.com/request_count" AND metric.labels.response_code_class = "5xx"
